title: phymatodes quercifolia
intro: Phymatodes quercifolia, also known as Riddell's ash or blue ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to North America. The medicinal properties of Phymatodes quercifolia lie in its bark, which has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The health benefits associated with Phymatodes quercifolia include its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Phymatodes quercifolia include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
